text.title: Tailwind Design Tokens Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every tailwind-design-tokens application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Separate primitive tokens (raw values: #1A73E8, 16px) from semantic tokens (color.action.primary, spacing.md). Tailwind config exposes ONLY semantic tokens; primitives stay in CSS variables.
rule.rationale: Primitive-only configs make rebranding impossible without code churn; semantic-only configs lose the raw layer required for theming.

rule.statement: Tailwind theme values MUST reference `var(--token-name)`. Hardcoded hex or pixel values in tailwind.config.js are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Hardcoded values require a build for any change and cannot be overridden at runtime for theming.

rule.statement: To support Tailwind opacity modifiers (`bg-primary/50`), colour CSS variables MUST be declared in RGB channel format (e.g. `--color-primary: 26 115 232`) and Tailwind colors MUST use the RGB variable with `<alpha-value>`.
rule.rationale: Opacity modifiers require channel-form values; hex strings break opacity silently.

rule.statement: Light-mode tokens declared at `:root`; dark-mode tokens declared under `[data-theme="dark"]` OR `@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark)`. NEVER mix Tailwind's `dark:` variant with raw CSS-variable tokens without explicit alpha-channel setup.
rule.rationale: Mixing strategies causes silent failures where dark mode is detected but tokens never switch.

rule.statement: Any token-mapped class assembled dynamically (string concatenation like `bg-${color}`) MUST be added to Tailwind's safelist. JIT cannot statically analyse template strings.
rule.rationale: Without safelist the class is purged at build and the runtime style does not exist.

rule.statement: Tailwind major version MUST be known and matched. v3 uses `tailwind.config.js`; v4 uses CSS-first `@theme` directive. Cross-version configs are silently ignored.
rule.rationale: v3 config in a v4 project ships nothing and produces no error.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
